message:
fuzz(corpus): expand pdu_stream_decode seeds from libfuzzer run

Adds 1032 new seed inputs to fuzz/corpus/pdu_stream_decode/ (mostly
small — 1–200 bytes each, typical for fuzzer-discovered interesting
inputs) harvested from a libfuzzer run against the allocation-bounded
decoder fix from 6dc90fd9 (`fix(codec): bound deserialization
allocations in pdu stream decode (ft-y87lf)`).

Keeping them checked in matches the precedent set by 3595322f
(`fuzz(corpus): expand pdu_stream_decode + termwiz_csi_parser seeds`)
and serves two purposes:

1. **Regression coverage** — every seed that discovered a crash, edge
   branch, or previously-unseen coverage is preserved; future fuzz
   runs resume from the full accumulated corpus rather than
   rediscovering the same inputs.

2. **Reproducibility** — CI or another developer running
   `cargo fuzz run pdu_stream_decode` gets the same starting corpus,
   so a crash found locally reproduces against the same seeds.

No code changes — only new files under `fuzz/corpus/pdu_stream_decode/`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
